personality_one_liner: A highly functional, poster-like utility system designed for field clarity, driven by industrial high-contrast structural borders and stark edge-to-edge layouts.

spacing.rule: Use rigid grids, edge-to-edge sections, and exposed spacing systems. Avoid soft floating cards. Whitespace should be intentional and imbalanced.

do's: Use solid surfaceSecondary or surfaceInverse for elevated sticky bars instead of glass.
dont's: No blur, no frosted glass. Brutalist systems rely on solid structural layers and sharp contrast.

screens.name: Home
screens.purpose: Role-based command center showing live metrics for contractors and timeline progress for clients.

states.loading: Centered oversized Archivo text 'LOADING...' with a thick border loading bar.
states.empty: Sharp industrial graphic or blueprint image, hard-aligned text 'NO ACTIVE SITES', and a solid industrial orange CTA.
states.error: Signal red background section, bold mono error code, and a sharp black 'RETRY' button.

screens.layout: Edge-to-edge rigid grid. Top header features a bold title. Followed by oversized metric blocks (e.g., Active Workers, Pending Quotes) surrounded by 2pt solid black borders. Use sticky primary CTA at the bottom ('NEW LOG').

screens.name: Projects Detail
screens.purpose: Comprehensive view of a single construction site, schedules, and progress photos.

states.empty: Blueprint image placeholder, 'NO MEDIA LOGGED' label.
states.error: Error message in sharp bordered container.

screens.layout: Hero image at the top layered with a vertical gradient scrim and large Archivo text. Sections separated by thick black 2pt dividers. Tabular task lists using IBM Plex Sans. Segmented controls for switching between 'Timeline', 'Media', and 'Invoices'.

screens.name: Crew & Inventory
screens.purpose: Track worker attendance and material quantities in a dense, scannable format.
screens.layout: Split-panel layout or sticky top filters. List rows feature mono-spaced metadata for timestamps and material counts. Rows are separated by 0.5pt dividers but encapsulated in a heavy 2pt bordered main container.

screens.name: AI Assistant & Estimates
screens.purpose: Mechanical, structured chat interface for generating cost estimates and looking up building codes.

states.empty: Large prompt suggestions in 2pt outlined boxes.

screens.layout: Chat bubbles replaced by sharp, hard-aligned rectangular blocks. Exposed system states (e.g., 'GENERATING INVOICE...'). Input area is a rigid full-width bordered container above the keyboard.

profile_worker.alt: Construction worker wearing a safety vest and helmet

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the '5 Brutalist Mobile LIGHT' aesthetic. This means ZERO border radius on all components, buttons, and images.
Use 2pt black borders (`borderStrong`: #111111) aggressively for structural hierarchy, cards, and segmented controls.
Never use shadows or glassmorphism. Rely entirely on solid color flipping, stark contrast, and hard dividers.
Typography is critical: Use Archivo for all headers, metrics, tabs, and large UI text to provide a poster-like structure. Use IBM Plex Sans for tables, inventory lists, timestamps, and body text.
Ensure large, comfortable touch targets for field workers (min 56pt row heights), but keep the visual containers rigid.
For the AI Assistant screen, avoid soft/rounded chat bubbles. Use sharp, full-width blocks for messages to match the operational, mechanical vibe.
Ensure the hero images utilize the required top-to-bottom vertical gradient scrim when overlaying text for readability.
Always maintain a sticky primary CTA on screens with major actions (e.g., 'New Log', 'Generate Quote') encapsulated in a solid container, not floating.
